strengths: 20 years software engineering experience exceeding 15-year minimum, with Staff-level progression across full-stack and infrastructure domains
Currently working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, directly relevant to the position's AI focus
Demonstrates leadership capacity through founding GISCollective, teaching workshops (Web Internals, Selenium, TDD), and public speaking at conferences
concerns: No Master's degree or PhD in AI/ML (preferred qualification); holds two Bachelor's degrees but neither in AI-related field
AI expertise appears recent and infrastructure-focused; no demonstrated track record of building AI-powered products or platforms prior to current role (April 2025)
No evidence of advising customers/partners on AI adoption strategy or recognized thought leadership in AI space; career is generalist rather than AI-specialized
response.justification: The candidate brings substantial software engineering breadth (20 years, Staff-level positions, multiple domains) and is currently working on AI-agent infrastructure. However, the role explicitly targets Director-level "AI Strategy" expertise with demonstrated AI products and customer advisory experience, preferably with advanced degree in AI. The candidate's AI engagement is recent and infrastructure-centric rather than strategic or product-focused. While strong on technical foundation and current AI relevance, the gap in prior AI expertise, advanced credentials, and strategic AI background indicates better fit for Staff-level AI infrastructure engineer than Principal-level AI Strategy role.
